articles.title: Why are cells small?
articles.summary: Cells come in a wide range of shapes and sizes, with the smallest human cell being just 30 µm³ while the largest oocyte is over 4 million µm³, spanning five orders of magnitude. The size difference can be attributed to evolution's need for each cell type to have the optimal volume for its function, but physics also plays a significant role in determining cell size limits. The surface area-to-volume ratio and diffusion rates dictate how quickly molecules can move within cells, with larger molecules diffusing slower than smaller ones due to their increased interaction with obstacles and other molecules.

articles.title: Stop the Apple Music app from launching
articles.summary: The Apple Music app appears to be launching even when the system music app is running, causing issues with keyboard control. This happens because the Music Decoy app has a bundle identifier of com.apple.Music and makes the system think it's already running by sharing this identifier. To disable this, developers can configure the Music Decoy app to launch another app when the Play button is pressed or reset its configuration. The issue is caused by the Remote Control Daemon (rcd), which checks for playing audio apps before launching the system music app. Developers can use tools like launchctl to unload the rcd plist file or alternative methods such as quitting the Music Decoy app manually, but these solutions have their own limitations and drawbacks.

articles.title: How much do amd64 microarchitecture levels help in Go?
articles.summary: The Go toolchain exposes a v1 through v4 ladder for AMD64 microarchitecture levels, allowing developers to target older instruction sets while still compiling on newer hardware. This means that a realistic "use everything this CPU offers" target today would need at least a v5, and the entire scheme should be replaced by finer-grained feature detection. The Go toolchain provides a way to set the GOAMD64 environment variable to use everything up to and including AVX2, raising questions about how much gain is actually made from compiling at each level.
